medical_history.chief_complaint: acute weakness and clumsiness of the left hand, dysarthria and gait disturbance
medical_history.history_of_present_illness: One year preceding her death this 69-year-old woman developed acute weakness and clumsiness of the left hand, dysarthria and gait disturbance. These symptoms disappeared within a few hours except for a mild clumsiness. Later her daughter noted difficulties of her mother in orientation in her own flat, which had become untidy. She also noted short-term memory deficits and general slowing of her mother. At the time of clinical presentation, 1 month later, she had mild cognitive impairment, especially short-term memory deficits and gave incoherent answers. The neurological examination showed mild left facial paresis, latent paresis of the left hand, mild dysarthria, dysdiadochokinesia and mild gait ataxia. She had arterial hypertension. She had been seriously injured in a car accident a few years before clinical admission.
medical_history.past_medical_history: arterial hypertension, seriously injured in a car accident a few years before clinical admission
medical_history.family_history: Her mother had died at the age of 90 years. She had a urinary bladder carcinoma; no dementia was reported. Her father had died at the age of 45 years of bronchial hemorrhage. Her younger sister (67 years) has been suffering from a psychiatric disorder without dementia or neurological deficits for about 5 years. Her brother (62 years) is healthy. She has two healthy daughters aged 41 and 38.

physical_examination.general_examination: mild left facial paresis, latent paresis of the left hand, mild dysarthria, dysdiadochokinesia and mild gait ataxia
physical_examination.specialty_examination: The cerebral MRT showed vascular lacunar lesions of the right basal ganglia, the genu of the internal capsule and periventricular white matter. The EEG initially showed diffuse slowing and progressed to triphasic and tetraphasic sharp-wave discharges as the disease progressed. The CSF showed a mild blood-CSF barrier disturbance, elevated neuron-specific enolase (74 ng/ml) and 14-3-3 proteins were detectable.

laboratory_examinations.findings: Mild blood-CSF barrier disturbance, elevated neuron-specific enolase (74 ng/ml), and detectable 14-3-3 proteins
Single point mutation at codon 208 (R208H) of the PRNP gene, homozygosity for methionine at codon 129

Typical PrPSc pattern type 1 with an additional 17 kDa fragment. Glycosylation ratios: frontal lobe - diglycosylated 16%, monoglycosylated 34.9%, unglycosylated 21 kDa 42.5%, unglycosylated 17 kDa 6.3%; cerebellum - diglycosylated 22.6%, monoglycosylated 38.4%, unglycosylated 21 kDa 32.5%, unglycosylated 17 kDa 6.4%

radiographic_examinations.findings: Vascular lacunar lesions of the right basal ganglia, the genu of the internal capsule, and periventricular white matter

other_tests.findings: Initial diffuse slowing progressing to triphasic and tetraphasic sharp-wave discharges
other_tests.changes_over_time: Progressed from diffuse slowing to triphasic and tetraphasic sharp-wave discharges

other_tests.findings: Severe spongiform changes in all layers of the cerebral cortex, severe neuronal degeneration, astrocytic gliosis, rarefaction of cerebral white matter, ballooned neurons in the deeper layers of the frontal cortex, nerve cell loss, gliosis, and spongiform changes in the basal ganglia and thalamus. Minor spongiform changes in the hippocampus, nearly spared brainstem with astrocytic gliosis. Severe spongiform changes in the molecular layer of the cerebellar cortex, degeneration of the granular cell layer, loss of Purkinje cells, mild atrophy of cerebellar white matter, minor vacuolar change, nerve cell loss, and gliosis in the dentate nucleus. Remote ischemic infarct in the right putamen and status lacunaris of the basal ganglia

Synaptic PrP staining in all layers of the cerebral and cerebellar cortex, predilection for deep cortical layers of the cerebrum and deep and middle cortical layer of the entorhinal and transentorhinal cortex. Synaptic staining in the hippocampus, amygdala, basal ganglia, thalamus, and tectum. Coarse staining pattern in the thalamus and granular layer of the cerebellum. Negative for PrP in the pituitary gland and ganglion Gasseri. AT8-positive NFT and neurons with stained cytoplasm in the CA1 region, AT8-positive inclusions in oligodendrocytes and astrocytes in the CA1 region and external layer of the entorhinal cortex, numerous AT8-positive tiny granules in the CA1 region and entorhinal cortex, felt-like network in the alveus. aB-crystallin-immunoreactive ballooned neurons in the cortex of the cingulate gyrus, frontal and temporal lobe, subiculum, entorhinal and transentorhinal cortex, hippocampus, amygdala, putamen, and claustrum
